data.title: Healthcare Triangle, Inc. (HCTI) to present at Deal Flow Discovery Conference in Atlantic City. Nasdaq: HCTI.
data.description: Pleasanton, California – On January 28, 2026, Healthcare Triangle, Inc. (Nasdaq: HCTI) – a prominent provider of digital transformation services for the healthcare and life sciences sectors – disclosed its involvement in the Deal Flow Discovery Conference. The conference is scheduled to take place soon, highlighting the company's dedication to networking and exploring potential business opportunities.

data.title: US insurer Elevance Health (NYSE: ELV) faces lower premium forecast for 2026 after reporting $49.3B revenue in Q4 2025, up 10% YoY. Operating revenue reached $197.6B in 2025, up 13%. Adjusted earnings were $3.33/share, beating consensus $3.10. The benefit expense ratio was 93.5% in Q4, up 110 basis points, driven by medical cost trends. Operating expense ratio was 11%.
data.description: Elevance Health Inc. (NYSE: ELV) shares dropped post a mixed Q4 2025 earnings report and a grim fiscal 2026 outlook. Q4 2025 revenues stood at $49.3 billion, climbing 10% YoY, slightly under the $49.82 billion consensus. Operating revenue in 2025 was $197.6 billion, up 13%, propelled by higher premium yields in Health Benefits sector, acquisitions' contributions, and Medicare Advantage growth, offset by Medicaid business member attrition. Adjusted earnings were $3.33 per share, surpassing the estimated $3.10. Benefit expense ratio was 93.5% in Q4, up 110 basis points, due to increased medical costs mainly in ACA and seasonal Medicare Part D changes from Inflation Reduction Act. For the year, the benefit expense ratio was 90%, up 150 basis points due to heightened medical cost trends. The operating expense ratio was 11% in Q4. Elevance Health stock recovered some of its premarket losses.

data.title: Reliance Global Group prices $2 million public offering of 7,407,408 shares of common stock at $0.27/share with associated warrants.
data.description: Reliance Global Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: EZRA) based in Lakewood, NJ, disclosed today the pricing details of a public offering. The offering consists of 7,407,408 shares of common stock (or pre-funded warrants instead) and warrants allowing the purchase of up to 14,814,816 common stock shares. The combined price for the public offering is set at $0.27 per share (or pre-funded warrant) alongside the associated warrants. The exercise price for the warrants is at $0.27 per share, with exercise eligibility at issuance and a two-year expiration period. The closure of this offering is slated for around January 29, 2026, pending the fulfillment of standard closing prerequisites.

data.title: Impact.com introduces Geodesix, connecting publishers with AI systems through a data marketplace, addressing AI economy issues.
data.description: A company based in New York, impact.com, revealed the introduction of Geodesix, a data marketplace offering licensed commerce content and a transparent compensation model for publishers. This initiative by Impact Labs, the company's innovation and venture division, aims to rectify a discrepancy in the AI sector. The platform caters to AI systems by providing access to licensed commerce content with clear terms on publisher payment. This move is significant as it addresses an emerging challenge in the industry as large language models depend more on such data.

data.title: NARMA strengthens US presence in response to FCC's Covered List expansion for UAS; foreign-produced drones face hurdles.
data.description: In Corpus Christi, Texas on January 28, 2026, NARMA, a company specializing in dual tilt-rotor VTOL UAS, revealed plans to enhance its operations in the United States following the recent expansion of the "Covered List" by the FCC. This expansion impacts certain foreign-produced UAS entering the market. Despite these challenges, NARMA is pushing ahead to solidify its position with a focus on innovation and growth within the U.S. It aims to capitalize on this opportunity for expansion despite the obstacles faced by other UAS manufacturers.

data.title: LEPAS ships 500 L4 vehicles to South Africa from China, accelerating global market expansion.
data.description: In Taicang, China, on January 26, 2026, the newly launched NEV brand LEPAS finished its export process at Taicang Port. More than 500 LEPAS L4 vehicles were loaded and sent to Durban Port, South Africa. This marks the initial shipment to South Africa, a pivotal hub for the company's expansion efforts.

data.title: FSA Store® clarifies March 15 FSA grace period, 70% at risk of losing average $463 unused funds to deadline.
data.description: Experts from FSA Store® have addressed misconceptions about deadlines to assist about 70% of account holders in preventing the loss of an average of $463 in unused funds due to missing the deadline. data.title: Air Canada broadens winter network - adds Quito, year-round Manchester/Copenhagen service, and Calgary-Mexico routes.
data.description: In a recent announcement, Air Canada revealed its strategic expansion of the winter network in 2026. The expansion includes significant updates such as the addition of Quito to its network, year-round services to Manchester and Copenhagen, as well as the introduction of two sun destinations from Calgary. Additionally, the airline has increased its winter services to Latin American destinations. These developments come after Air Canada's previous announcement this month regarding the exclusive launch of non-stop flights from North America to Sapporo. Overall, this winter, travelers can expect more options and convenience with Air Canada's expanded network.
